title: Determination of Stability Constants of 4-(o-Methoxyphenyl) thiosemicarbazide Complexes with Cobalt(II), Nickel(II) and Oxovanadium(IV)
author: S. B. SAXBNA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Applied Chemistry, Madhav Institute of Technology and Science, Gwalior-474 005 

  Manuscript received 30 May 1983, revised 3 January 1985, accepted 29 April 1985  

 Potentiometric studies on metal complexes of Co 2+ , Ni 2+  and VO  2 +  with 4-( o -meth­oxyphenyl)thiosemicarbazide have been investigated. The proton-ligand dissociation constants ( pK  1  and pK 2  ) of the reagent and the formation constants of IN metal com­plexes have been determined by Bjerrum's method at 37± 1° and at three ionic concen­trations, 0.05, 0.10 and 0.20  M  in 40 : 60 percent (v/v) ethanol-water medium. The stoichiometry of the metal and ligand intraction was found to be 1 : 2 from Job's conti­nuous variation method.

 Potentiometric studies on metal complexes of Co 2+ , Ni 2+  and VO  2 +  with 4-( o -meth­oxyphenyl)thiosemicarbazide have been investigated. The proton-ligand dissociation constants ( pK  1  and pK 2  ) of the reagent and the formation constants of IN metal com­plexes have been determined by Bjerrum's method at 37± 1° and at three ionic concen­trations, 0.05, 0.10 and 0.20  M  in 40 : 60 percent (v/v) ethanol-water medium. The stoichiometry of the metal and ligand intraction was found to be 1 : 2 from Job's conti­nuous variation method.
